title: hopea rugifolia
intro: Hopea rugifolia, also known as the rough-barked hopea, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Hopea rugifolia lie in its bark, which contains compounds that have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it a potential natural remedy for various health issues. The health benefits associated with Hopea rugifolia include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to help manage diabetes and hypertension. Traditionally, the bark of Hopea rugifolia is used to prepare herbal remedies, such as decoctions and infusions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and alleviate various health conditions.
